programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes Ruby a surprising offer - first refusal to buy the E20 club. But can she persuade Sharon to sell? Ian gives Iqra and Keegan a dressing down after learning they have been using the restaurant kitchen for their own business(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob realises time is running out on the investigation and determines the key to finding the truth is to unlock Adam's long-lost memories, no matter how painful or traumatic. Meanwhile, Cassie starts to back away from Operation Vestal, spending time with Frank as he schools her in the art of being Lexie as she investigates the dead woman's friends(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast business presenter Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

Lisa encourages Mel to see a grief counsellor, Mariam tells Honey she knows about Adam's affair and Dotty tries to sabotage Keegan's business. Sharon admits to Ben the real reason she is selling the club, leading him to share a secret that changes everything(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron makes a fatal mistake on Halloween, which leaves him forced to confront the ghosts of his own past. Jac feels threatened when Stacey reveals that she and Johnny are planning to leave Holby and start a new life in Edinburgh - but when she responds with a threat, it results in an even bigger revelation. Dominic gets the wrong idea about co-parenting Lofty's baby(n)

programme.desc: Cassie immerses herself in a dangerous undercover assignment, but finds herself seduced into the lifestyle of the people she is investigating. She and Frank discover that Whitethorn House has many enemies, who hate the residents both for the house's history of colonialism and cruelty and the obstacle they represent to the community's prosperity in the present. Rob reaches out to the woman in his recovered memories, but realises he is unable to pursue his prime suspect without giving away the secret of his identity(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: The case of gay New York teenager Abel Cedeno, who fatally stabbed a classmate and claimed he was acting in self-defence after enduring years of homophobic bullying. The ensuing trial created a sensation in the press and divided the city. The programme follows Abel, his victim's family, and lawyers on both sides of the trial as they attempt to make their case under intense public and media scrutiny(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Olympic athlete Jenny Jones joins Russell Kane for his latest assignment on a Costa Rican island. They soon discover that they face being cooked slowly by the scorching temperatures - if the large number of deadly scorpions and snakes don't get them first. They seek help from the internet to build a raft, fish using spears, and behead the region's toughest sea creature(n)

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a three-bedroom house in Worcester and Dion Dublin sees a tiny basement flat in Liverpool. Plus, a revisit to a London property first featured in 2016(n)

programme.desc: An audacious claim for compensation ends up costing one man a lot more than he bargained for when CCTV shows what really happened(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform, including an old oak table and Victorian sofa(n)

programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones , Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his phone on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. Once he returns to the house to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the house is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed(n)

programme.title: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein (1994)
programme.desc: An idealistic but obsessive scientist builds a living creature from the body parts of corpses, then abandons his creation to its fate. However, the monster survives, and tracks his creator down to make a sinister request - with terrifying repercussions if his demands are not met. Gothic horror, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Robert De Niro and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin serves up some winning houses for tennis-playing town-seekers keen to test out Shaftesbury as their perfect Dorset town and have £460,000 to make the move. They quickly discover it's a top choice, and not just because it's on a hill but because of a great social scene, high street independents, festival events and stunning green spaces(n)

programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.desc: The probate detectives investigate a man from Margate, who led a life shrouded in mystery. Plus, a Cambridgeshire man who was a legend in tug-of-war(n)

programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family(n)

programme.desc: Actors David Bradley and Nigel Harman travel to Oxfordshire and Somerset in search of antiques, assisted by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Christina Trevanion. Nigel takes a shine to a domestic appliance of yesteryear, and David comes across an item that resonates with one of the characters he has played(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the final first-round heat as George Orwell fans the Orwellians take on gin drinkers the Junipers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Mary the Mutant, Krusty the Clown, Chandler Bing, and Scaramanga(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's penultimate first-round match takes place, with four students from Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford pitting their wits against a team from Downing College, Cambridge in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Who Are You Calling Fat?
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nine people who live with obesity move in together to explore what it means to be larger-bodied in Britain today, exploring, examining whether they should love their bodies and embrace their size, or strive to get healthy and lose weight. In the first edition, they meet and settle into a house in the Oxfordshire countryside and begin to share their personal stories. But when Del starts to tell them about the bariatric surgery that he feels has saved his life, Victoria accuses him of having stomach amputation surgery and the battle lines are drawn(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Kevin reluctantly hosts a last-minute Halloween party, while Julia takes the kids trick-or-treating in an attempt to meet a celebrity who has just moved into the neighbourhood. Liz tags along as she has been stood up by a date and Anne is in attendance trying to make sure that the kids are safe - but by the time they reach Kevin's house one of the children is missing(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to novelist Margaret Atwood as her sequel to The Handmaid's Tale - The Testaments - is published, Their far-reaching conversation touches upon how Gilead has changed almost 35 years on from the original book and how the cloak featured in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale has become a symbol of protest around the world. Plus, Atwood shares her responses to the current political climate, about the accusation that she is a 'bad feminist'; and the hype surrounding the release of this new book(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Matt Baker goes on the hunt for the elusive adder, and finds out about a project in the forest that is teaching locals heritage crafts. Margherita Taylor meets the brass band making a 'big noise' in the area. She also explores the extraordinary Puzzlewood to discover how a landscape like this is protected. Adam Henson is on his farm keeping everything crossed as Lexie, his Suffolk Punch mare, gets a pregnancy scan(n)

programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travels the 400 miles from Amarillo, Texas to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure(n)

However, a fresh danger is posed by the arrival of hunters in the forest and Gordon decides to walk with the animals to protect them(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Biologist Lynn Rogers uses a controversial technique - giving food to wild black bears to gain their trust - and follows a family of four of the animals over a year in and around the forests of northern Minnesota. She aims to provide an intimate portrait of the lives and habits of these creatures(n)

programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin, in which a photographer and an upcycler are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles in Stafford(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present a week of programmes showcasing wildlife around the UK live from the Dell of Abernethy in the Cairngorms. The first episode features a guest appearance by astronaut Tim Peake, who explains how the seasonal cycle was influenced by a giant collision in space billions of years ago, and a Yorkshire GP who has befriended a colony of North Sea seals. Plus, the results of citizen science project Gardenwatch, in which members of the public were asked to survey the animals in their gardens(n)

programme.title: Who Are You Calling Fat?
programme.desc: Part two of two. The group attends a dance class, a comedy gig and a family barbecue, and Babs and Victoria clash over their differing opinions on beauty. The members of the group also delve into their past, considering how they became obese, and receive the results of their DNA tests - which yield some surprising results for Jed. The director of charity Obesity UK makes the case that the NHS' treatment of people who struggle with their weight is often short-sighted(n)

programme.title: Babies: Their Wonderful World
programme.desc: Paediatrician Guddi Singh looks at how infants build social relationships in the first two years of their lives, including the instinct to respond to faces, what makes them laugh and how they learn to empathise with others. Neuroscientist Sarah Lloyd-Fox uses a portable brain scanner to analyse activity in one week-old Arthur's brain as he looks at images of toys and faces, while Nathalia Gjersoe sets up an experiment with good and bad puppets to find out when babies make decisions about who to trust(n)

A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels into the secret heart of the Rough Bounds of Knoydart, by way of Loch nan Umbh, Loch Morar, Loch Nevis and Loch Arkaig. He lifts the lid on the area's secret connection with the celebrated SOE (Special Operations Executive), which Churchill hoped would 'set Europe ablaze'(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present live from the Cairngorms National Park, where overnight events have already created dramas. The team look at how this year's seasonal changes have shaped Autumn 2019. Meanwhile, Michaela embarks on a wild goose chase, and the camera team are on a mission to find a deer rut deep in the Abernethy forest(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: In a residential Edinburgh street one night, two brothers run into and kill an old man. Uninsured and under the influence, record shop owner Jake persuades high-flying lawyer Max to cover up the act. But their actions soon come back to haunt them. Thriller, starring Mark Bonnar and Jamie Sives, with Emun Elliott and Sian Brooke(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the fifth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Fall
programme.desc: A mob's punishment of a lone man proves cruel and unusual in this nightmarish short film, written and directed by Jonathan Glazer(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate a gang-related murder on Clapham Common in 1953 that left 17-year-old John Beckley dead. Several young men were initially arrested and charged, only one - Michael Davies - was found guilty of murder and sentenced to death. Michael's punishment was reduced to life imprisonment and now, more than six decades later, his niece, Sharon is looking for closure. Michael protested his innocence on his release from prison, but he was never exonerated - can his relative finally clear his name?(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart combines biography, art and the history of fashion to explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, starting with Charles II(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night(n)

programme.title: Hidden History: The Lost Portraits of Bradford
programme.desc: Shanaz Gulzar sets out to identify and track down some of the people in a collection of portraits that were rescued from a skip outside a small photographic studio in Bradford, west Yorkshire, 30 years ago. Many of the photos depict new arrivals from the Asian subcontinent, eastern Europe and the Caribbean, attracted by the offer of work in wool mills, and together they record the changing face of a British industrial city in the middle of the 20th century(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Jennifer Deschamps investigates the events leading up to bankruptcy of the global financial services firm in 2008, telling the stories of several former employees whose professional lives were ruined when they attempted to raise the alarm(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of the Qing, China's last empire, which lasted from 1644 till 1911, doubled the size of the country by including Xinjiang, Mongolia and Tibet, and tolerated a diversity of cultures and religions, including Islam. Michael explores the Qing's heritage with visits to a women's mosque, storytelling houses and a traditional printing house to see how all 48,000 Tang Poems were reproduced using hand-carved wood blocks. He also joins audiences to watch open-air opera in the snow and hears about the first Opium War, when a British expedition destroyed the Qing navy and extracted territory and trading rights(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Modern World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes retraces the lives of three great thinkers - Karl Marx, Frederick Nietzsche and Sigmund Freud - whose ideas shaped the modern world, beginning with Marx. Born to an affluent Prussian family, Marx became an angry, idealistic radical, constantly on the run for his political agitating and incendiary writing. His masterpiece Das Capital was largely overlooked in his lifetime, and only 11 people attended his funeral, yet his ideas would generate one of the most influential and divisive ideologies in history(n)

programme.title: All Aboard! The Canal Trip
programme.desc: Cameras capture a journey down one of Britain's most historic waterways, the Kennet and Avon Canal, filmed in real time to allow viewers to take in the images and sounds of the British countryside, spot wildlife and glimpse life on the towpath as if they were there. Archive stills and graphics are used to deliver salient facts about the canal and its social history(n)

programme.title: Hidden History: The Lost Portraits of Bradford
programme.desc: Shanaz Gulzar sets out to identify and track down some of the people in a collection of portraits that were rescued from a skip outside a small photographic studio in Bradford, west Yorkshire, 30 years ago. Many of the photos depict new arrivals from the Asian subcontinent, eastern Europe and the Caribbean, attracted by the offer of work in wool mills, and together they record the changing face of a British industrial city in the middle of the 20th century(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Fusing biography, art and the history of fashion, Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore. This time, Van Eyck's Arnolfini Portrait(n)

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water(n)

programme.title: Murder in Soho: Who Killed Freddie Mills?
programme.desc: With access to eight hours of previously unseen home movies, this documentary offers an intimate portrayal of boxing hero Freddie Mills, who rose from the humble surroundings of the fairground boxing booth to become light-heavyweight champion of the world.In July 1965 he was found shot dead in the back seat of his car. Fifty years after his death his relatives still challenge the coroner's verdict of suicide, maintaining he was murdered(n)

programme.title: Metamorphosis - The Science of Change
programme.desc: David Malone explores the processes involved in metamorphosis, the means through which one being transforms into another, and investigates why this distinctive change seems to have such a powerful grasp on the human imagination. Along the way, he highlights examples throughout the scope of historical fiction, from the epics of Ovid to the surreal short stories of Franz Kafka and the fantasy heroics of Marvel's X-Men(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The first of two programmes telling the story of the Brexit negotiations, capturing off the record conversations of the European negotiators as they devise a strategy. The film documents how the Europeans' respect for a formidable negotiating opponent turns into frustration, irritation and ultimately ridicule as the British fail to present either a united front or a clear goal for the future(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The second of two programmes following the work of Guy Verhofstadt, the European Parliament's Brexit coordinator, as he and his team devise a strategy for dealing with the British(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Fusing biography, art and the history of fashion, Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore. This time, Van Eyck's Arnolfini Portrait(n)

programme.title: Korea: the Never Ending War
programme.desc: Retracing the history of the Cold War in Korea, that resulted in millions of deaths, brought the world to the brink of nuclear war and continues to shape history to this day(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, this week providing a portrait of a working man(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: A dramatised account of the key moments in the lives of Henry VIII's wives, combining drama with the presenter's own contemporary historical comment. In this episode, Lucy follows Catherine of Aragon through her emotional and physical struggles to give Henry the heir he so desperately needs to continue his dynasty. When Catherine finally gives birth to a baby girl, Henry's eye wanders to the ladies at court - first Mary Boleyn, and then to her sister, Anne(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Emma Dabiri and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit museums as they track down previously unknown paintings by some of the world's greatest artists. In Oxford's Bodleian Library, Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds a finely painted portrait of Merton College fellow George Oakley Aldrich by an unknown artist, which he believes could be a piece of work by Pompeo Batoni(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Historian Janina Ramirez and art critic Alastair Sooke embark on cultural city breaks, offering insights into the most famous locations and uncovering hidden gems and untold stories to reveal how religion, revolution and trailblazing individuals can shape the art - and soul - of a metropolis. They begin by examining the art and design of the Dutch capital Amsterdam, along the way discovering how its culture reflects the liberal attitudes, appetite for global trade and love of home comforts that helped shape the character of this city(n)

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Letters: A Monarch Unveiled
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part programme, biographer AN Wilson explores the personal life of Queen Victoria through her journals and letters, examining the monarch's closest relationships in the four decades following Prince Albert's death to chart how her life changed. He looks at her controversial friendship with her servant John Brown and her unconventional behaviour around Indian waiter Abdul Karim, and also reveals how she gained greater freedom in her widowhood. Includes readings by Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.title: GiggleQuiz

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mrs Juniper has an old cassette tape of her choir. When the kids accidentally unwind it to make a sculpture, Molly forms a choir to sing for Mrs Juniper while Bob fixes things(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After finding a paper cone useful for 'loud talking', Mr Onion arrives at a sleepy castle(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: On their way to tea with the Iron Chicken, Major, Mother, Tiny and Small spot a fascinating new planet. Major insists on exploring it and all become lost inside. A passing Skymoo spots the empty music boat and brings Granny to the rescue(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: The country is gripped by Banksy fever following the discovery of one of his artworks in Port Talbot, while the staff of Wow Wales! settle into their new premises. Return of the comedy about a tourism agency, starring Sally Phillips(n)

programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesar brothers Kheerat and Jags arrive in Walford, although as Ben squares up to them, he is quickly embarrassed to learn they have more important business. Lisa encourages Mel to see a grief counsellor, Mariam tells Honey she knows about Adam's affair and Dotty tries to sabotage Keegan's business. Sharon admits to Ben the real reason she is selling the club, leading him to share a secret that changes everything(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron makes a fatal mistake on Halloween, which leaves him forced to confront the ghosts of his own past. Jac feels threatened when Stacey reveals that she and Johnny are planning to leave Holby and start a new life in Edinburgh - but when she responds with a threat, it results in an even bigger revelation. Dominic gets the wrong idea about co-parenting Lofty's baby(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Cassie immerses herself in a dangerous undercover assignment, but finds herself seduced into the lifestyle of the people she is investigating. She and Frank discover that Whitethorn House has many enemies, who hate the residents both for the house's history of colonialism and cruelty and the obstacle they represent to the community's prosperity in the present. Rob reaches out to the woman in his recovered memories, but realises he is unable to pursue his prime suspect without giving away the secret of his identity(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Miss UK Beauty first runner-up 2015 Alaw is seeking someone special. She's conscious her parents would love her to find a Welsh speaker as that is their family's first language. Standing at just 4ft 11ins, Alaw's got a tall boy on her wish-list. She admits she comes across as confident on the outside but is a bag of nerves on the inside - and that's exactly how she feels as the make-under begins. Like all the beauty queens, Alaw's supported by stylist Nathan who provides the voice of reason and a personal twist on a simple black dress bespoke to each girls(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: The case of gay New York teenager Abel Cedeno, who fatally stabbed a classmate and claimed he was acting in self-defence after enduring years of homophobic bullying. The ensuing trial created a sensation in the press and divided the city. The programme follows Abel, his victim's family, and lawyers on both sides of the trial as they attempt to make their case under intense public and media scrutiny(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Olympic athlete Jenny Jones joins Russell Kane for his latest assignment on a Costa Rican island. They soon discover that they face being cooked slowly by the scorching temperatures - if the large number of deadly scorpions and snakes don't get them first. They seek help from the internet to build a raft, fish using spears, and behead the region's toughest sea creature(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones , Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his phone on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. Once he returns to the house to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the house is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed(n)

programme.title: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein (1994)
programme.desc: An idealistic but obsessive scientist builds a living creature from the body parts of corpses, then abandons his creation to its fate. However, the monster survives, and tracks his creator down to make a sinister request - with terrifying repercussions if his demands are not met. Gothic horror, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Robert De Niro and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: Children in Need

Once he returns to the house to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the house is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed(n)

programme.title: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein (1994)

Gothic horror, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Robert De Niro and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: The team returns with more seasonal stories from around the UK, with John Craven exploring the countryside around the historic city of Oxford and Keeley Donovan heading to Anglesey to find out about the plight of red squirrels(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A prolific fraudster comes a cropper after targeting Royal Mail delivery vans, and a careless claimant is caught out when investigators discover his story is full of holes(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop.(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel confides in Zara when she asks his advice about Adam, while Al and Jimmi have an announcement to make, and Ayesha tries to convince a homeless boy to return to his mother(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Somerset for a couple who have a £310,000 budget, and finds out the endangered crane has been brought back from near extinction(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw set off on a search for items around the Trossachs in Stirlingshire, buying a spoon, a stool and a soda syphon along the way(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Family Footsteps
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews continues to explore the Smyths' historic connection with Russia when they discover another family member was operating in St Petersburg in the early 20th century. Ralph, the brother of Robert and Lizzie, was ostensibly a businessman but evidence points to him actually being a British spy. The Russian Revolution was to have devastating consequences for this branch of the family and sent them fleeing for their lives(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes Ruby a surprising offer - first refusal to buy the E20 club. But can she persuade Sharon to sell? Keanu pleads with Sharon to stop Louise moving to Portugal, and Mariam confronts Adam about Habiba, leading to an emotional heart-to-heart. Ian gives Iqra and Keegan a dressing down after learning they have been using the restaurant kitchen for their own business(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob realises time is running out on the investigation and determines the key to finding the truth is to unlock Adam's long-lost memories, no matter how painful or traumatic. Meanwhile, Cassie starts to back away from Operation Vestal, spending time with Frank as he schools her in the art of being Lexie as she investigates the dead woman's friends(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following a 60-year-old woman who takes on a a record-breaking attempt to row solo across the Atlantic Ocean, which will make her the oldest woman to do so, while back home, her sister is treated for a rare terminal cancer. The programme explores the mental and physical challenges both face(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The music and news stories of 1986, the year that saw a Unionist Day of Action descend into intimidation and street violence and a series of by-elections triggered by Unionist opposition to the previous year's Anglo-Irish Agreement. In June, the Northern Ireland Assembly is dissolved - and police are called in when Assemblymen refuse to leave Stormont(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast business presenter Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Aquaman star Jason Momoa talks about his part in futuristic TV drama See, Game of Thrones star Emilia Clarke promotes her romantic comedy Last Christmas, while Oscar-winner Regina King discusses her HBO series Watchmen. Top comic Ross Noble also secures a spot on Graham's sofa. Plus, American-Cuban singer-songwriter Camila Cabello performs Liar(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan goes on night patrol with police who are trying to catch criminals stealing contaminated meat, and Margherita Taylor encounters the robot farmers of the future(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three terraced properties including a former church are sold at auction in Beswick near Manchester, Swindon in Wiltshire and Preston in Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV exposes a chip shop worker's self-inflicted personal injury, a jewellery scam is exposed, and a motorist claiming against a bus company ends up regretting his road rage(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reveals how police used ingenious methods to track down a robber who had attacked and injured a train enthusiast. Plus, the mechanic who used his technical know-how to bring crimes at a garage to a sudden stop(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At the Royal Highland Centre near Edinburgh, experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper help two teams of sporty mothers and daughters to buy three items hoping to make a profit at auction(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky sounds out Ruhma for advice, and Daniel has a heart to heart with Jimmi. Rob and Emma are in for a shock when they attend a Taser training course(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Surrey to find a new home in Norfolk, and visits a local public garden to help out with some pruning ahead of the grand opening(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team restores A Lloyd Loom-style chair, a pair of bookcases, and a pine table with matching chairs that have been recovered from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Anita Manning visit the Islands and Lowlands of Scotland. Paul uncovers a festive antique, while Anita is caught by the gaze of a former Lord Provost of Kilmarnock(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesar brothers Kheerat and Jags arrive in Walford, although as Ben squares up to them, he is quickly embarrassed to learn they have more important business. Lisa encourages Mel to see a grief counsellor, Mariam tells Honey she knows about Adam's affair and Dotty tries to sabotage Keegan's business. Sharon admits to Ben the real reason she is selling the club, leading him to share a secret that changes everything(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron makes a fatal mistake on Halloween, which leaves him forced to confront the ghosts of his own past. Jac feels threatened when Stacey reveals that she and Johnny are planning to leave Holby and start a new life in Edinburgh - but when she responds with a threat, it results in an even bigger revelation. Dominic gets the wrong idea about co-parenting Lofty's baby(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Cassie immerses herself in a dangerous undercover assignment, but finds herself seduced into the lifestyle of the people she is investigating. She and Frank discover that Whitethorn House has many enemies, who hate the residents both for the house's history of colonialism and cruelty and the obstacle they represent to the community's prosperity in the present. Rob reaches out to the woman in his recovered memories, but realises he is unable to pursue his prime suspect without giving away the secret of his identity(n)

programme.title: Spotlight Special
programme.desc: A studio audience put questions to politicians and opinion-formers in a Brexit special. Noel Thompson chairs(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: The case of gay New York teenager Abel Cedeno, who fatally stabbed a classmate and claimed he was acting in self-defence after enduring years of homophobic bullying. The ensuing trial created a sensation in the press and divided the city. The programme follows Abel, his victim's family, and lawyers on both sides of the trial as they attempt to make their case under intense public and media scrutiny(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Olympic athlete Jenny Jones joins Russell Kane for his latest assignment on a Costa Rican island. They soon discover that they face being cooked slowly by the scorching temperatures - if the large number of deadly scorpions and snakes don't get them first. They seek help from the internet to build a raft, fish using spears, and behead the region's toughest sea creature(n)

programme.desc: In a journey that takes him from Rome, Germany and Geneva to the Outer Hebrides and across the island of Ireland, Alex O'Henley uncovers why the Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland became, to a large extent, Protestant while most of Ireland remained Catholic. programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition dedicated to the stories of twins separated at birth. Mary Holmes and Elaine Allin were born just after the Second World War and raised in the same neighbourhood in Chesterfield, but forbidden from seeing each other. It wasn't until they were in their thirties that the sisters were able to find each other - and even then it only happened by chance. There is also a look at the latest scientific research into what it is really like to be separated from a twin at a young age(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates a selection of clips submitted by members of the public, which display in cringe-worthy detail some of the many things that can go wrong while away on holiday. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Amid rumours that a giant fish is on the loose, biologist and monster-hunter Jeremy Wade sets out to track down the possible culprit. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? A trail of evidence leads Jeremy into an ancient rainforest, where an old adversary lies in wait. More than just a beast with a gruesome rap sheet, it's also the one river monster that he has thus far failed to catch(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Amid rumours that a giant fish is on the loose, biologist and monster-hunter Jeremy Wade sets out to track down the possible culprit. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? A trail of evidence leads Jeremy into an ancient rainforest, where an old adversary lies in wait. More than just a beast with a gruesome rap sheet, it's also the one river monster that he has thus far failed to catch(n)

programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.desc: Alf refuses to honour the OAP vouchers Brendan issued, Deirdre feels helpless as Tracy packs her bags and leaves with Craig, and Vera visits Terry in prison for the last time(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin goes to court, assuring Sally that he will be let off lightly. Don advises Denise to stop the search for the caller as the police might get involved(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy discovers Chris's deception, Michael finds he is unwelcome at Emmerdale, and Pollard and Elizabeth return home to an unpleasant surprise(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy makes a decision, and Rachel makes an angry move. Unknown to the Sugdens, Annie and Leonard are making plans for the future(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Arthur is attracted to Beryl, the clippie on his regular bus. However, she has only two interests in life - men with a fondness for athletics, and those with a good head of hair. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth makes a puzzling discovery, while Annie's actions shock her sons. At Home Farm, Zoe blames Frank for Archie's arrest, and Chris continues to plot against his father(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert refuses to let go of his new friend, there is a reconciliation for Kathy and Kim, and a treat in store for Turner on his birthday(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is annoyed that Steve has lost his licence, Joe urges Sally to leave Kevin and move in with him. Denise confronts Ivy and tells her she knows she has been making the calls(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise posts a cheque through Don's door for the money she owes him. Vera is thrilled to have Terry home and looks forward to having a happy Christmas(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. Murder mystery, guest starring Robert Glenister, with John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Gillian Kearney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don and Bet spend the night together, Denise accuses Don of making her life a misery, and Steve is upset to see Alison with another man(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel gets a job hostessing a party for a bathroom fittings company, Deirdre is horrified at the thought of having Craig around at Christmas. and Tanya sees Steve kissing Alison(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank's fears are confirmed by Lynn's revelation, Turner plucks up the courage to make a date, there is an unexpected candidate for a job, and a dinner party leaves Sarah envious(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel rebels over Joe's romance, there is mutiny in the Woolpack as Turner toes the line, but his foot is firmly down when Frank finally oversteps the mark(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A woman arrives at the depot to claim her lost property, but Stan and Jack have already eaten it. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan gets a new uniform and is proud to wear it - until he sits on a freshly painted chair. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah's at her wits' end, while Rachel reaches the end of her tether. Frank's on the wrong side of the law, and Elizabeth might take legal matters into her own hands(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is finally on the wagon, Steve finds himself on the rails, and Michael is put on the spot when Lynn reveals all(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise appreciates Don staying with her, Denise tells Bet she feels Tanya might be making the calls, and Maureen's revelation leaves Reg stunned(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Elaine has no memory of the party, Denise confronts Hanif and accuses him of making the calls, and Deirdre has enough of feeling in the way and asks Craig to leave(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A socialite is found dead next to the swimming pool of her manor house - following a series of threats against her. Barnaby and Troy are forced to dive deep into a dark world of adultery and deceit in affluent Midsomer Malham when they investigate. Mystery, starring John Nettles and Daniel Casey, with Charles Collingwood, Ann Bell and Miles Richardson(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullet, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Figgis dabbles in amateur psychiatry and deduces that Norman is suffering from sexual repression. Comedy, starring James Bolam and Christopher Strauli(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo receives death threats from an ex-convict with a vendetta - but he is not the only one in danger, as the vengeful crook targets the detective's niece on her wedding day(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: An ex-convict faces the prospect of getting locked up again after being charged with manslaughter, unless the sleuthing coroner can clear his name(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry's girlfriend Debbie starts work as a mobile hairdresser. Unfortunately the first house she attends is robbed, and she and Terry become suspects. When Arthur becomes involved, the police take over his lock-up. Vintage comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The university branch of a bank is robbed, and though Regan and the Flying Squad catch most of the gang a known killer and his sidekick remain inside holding the manager and two women hostage. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect Cowley's former girlfriend, now a controversial evangelist, from an assassin, but are unaware that the would-be hitman is right under their nose. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A diamond trail leads Templar to mannequins and murder. Classic adventure, starring Roger Moore as globe-trotting adventurer Simon Templar(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo tricks everyone into thinking he is corrupt in the hope that a drug kingpin will make a deal with him, and incriminate himself. But the gangster is preparing a trap of his own(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A series of accidents stops Quincy getting in to work on a typically busy morning, so Chief Coroner Hiro takes his place to examine the body of a suspected smuggler(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur persuades a reluctant Terry to make a comeback in the boxing ring to take on Jackie Wilson, the opponent he faced prior to losing his licence. However, Wilson's wife soon appears claiming her husband is unable to fight. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. programme.title: Building the Channel Tunnel: 25 Years On
programme.desc: The tunnel under the English Channel is an engineering triumph and a testament to what can be achieved when two nations work together. But what did it take to build the longest undersea tunnel ever constructed? The first of a two-part documentary, marking the 25th anniversary of its opening, focuses on the early stages of the build. The men and women who worked on the project share their memories of operating in the highly dangerous environment, former deputy prime minister John Prescott remembers the battles with contractors and banks to get the tunnel built and one-time Tomorrow's World presenter Judith Hann reveals how it captured the public's imagination(n)

programme.title: Then & Now

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild

Ben helps her chop wood and make her home baby-proof, while Annalisa goes busking to make a small living. They also go dumpster-diving in search of rubbish that can be put to good use to counter the amount of waste thrown away(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Presenter Michael Buerk looks at how the Victorians created what is now known as the modern home, exploring the huge rise in house-building during the period. He travels to Fakenham, Norfolk, to visit the last remaining gasworks in England, and discovers how the Victorians mastered the art of producing 'town gas' from coal. He also investigates how the kitchen was transformed with the advent of gas cookers, as more complex meals including the Sunday roast steadily became the norm across the nation(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a high-speed police interception unit. Officers Nick and Bob chase down a suspicious looking van with false registration plates. Meanwhile, Sophie and Adie hunt for a man linked to multiple sexual assault charges, and a reckless Audi driver is chased through Wakefield city centre(n)

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. A specialist counterfeit raid team targets businesses suspected of dealing fake goods, and energy theft enforcers raid a Liverpool takeaway which has registered a sudden drop in gas usage(n)

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends
programme.desc: Five men who think they are taking part in a reality TV show in Marbella are actually being watched by their partners, who are in control of the whole thing. In the third episode, The girls turn detective when they discover a secret text message has been sent, and all of the men could be involved. Later, they decide to call the boys from the middle of a nightclub(n)

His mission is to take on a group of marauding Seminole rebels, as well as the gun runners who have been supplying them with weapons, and rescue a group of settlers who have been taken hostage by the tribe. Western, starring Gary Cooper, Mari Aldon, Richard Webb, Ray Teal and Arthur Hunnicutt(n)

Michael Powell's Oscar-winning Second World War thriller, starring Anton Walbrook, Leslie Howard, Laurence Olivier, Glynis Johns and Niall MacGinnis(n)

Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender(n)

Thriller, starring Blake Lively and Oscar Jaenada(n)

Period horror, starring Anya Taylor-Joy and Ralph Ineson(n)

However, a group of renegade warriors are unwilling to surrender their arms and their actions prompt a bigoted government official to use brutal methods. Western, starring Dana Andrews, Kent Smith, Nestor Paiva and Henry Brandon(n)

programme.desc: A photographer is left housebound and in a wheelchair after breaking his leg in an accident. He passes the time watching the comings and goings of his neighbours in the apartment block opposite and becomes convinced one of them has committed a murder. However, he has trouble persuading anyone to believe his story. Hitchcock thriller, starring James Stewart, Grace Kelly, Raymond Burr, Thelma Ritter and Judith Evelyn(n)

When they escape from jail, he takes the law into his own hands and decides to hunt down and kill the fugitives. But over time, he begins to question whether his actions are right. Western, starring Gregory Peck, Joan Collins and Stephen Boyd(n)

These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
